commit.message:
fix: clear negative caches between runs, converge on stable API count

Rate-limited API failures were cached as negatives, preventing retries
and causing false convergence at 0 API calls. Now:
- Negative cache entries are cleared between loop iterations
- Convergence triggers when API call count is stable across 3 runs
  (not necessarily 0), reflecting genuinely missing data (~160)

commit.message:
Fix RS256 JWT signing: configure privateKey at JwtModule level

@nestjs/jwt's mergeJwtOptions() deletes privateKey from call-time
sign options before passing them to jsonwebtoken.sign(), so passing
privateKey per-call silently falls back to the module's symmetric
JWT_SECRET, which fails RS256 validation.

Fix: configure JwtModule with privateKey/publicKey from env vars and
remove the redundant per-call privateKey option in AuthService.

commit.message:
Migrate vault storage from localStorage to IndexedDB

localStorage has a hard 5-10MB browser limit. IndexedDB supports
hundreds of MB (Chrome: ~80% of disk, Firefox: ~50%, Safari: ~1GB),
enabling much larger attachments and vault data.

- Added IndexedDB key-value wrapper (idb.get/set/del/getAll/clear)
- Vault keys (salt, hash, encrypted data) now stored in IndexedDB
- One-time auto-migration moves existing localStorage vault data to IDB
- Cloud backup/restore updated to read/write vault keys from IndexedDB
- Clear cache now clears both localStorage and IndexedDB
- Small config keys (quotes, pomodoro, sync settings) stay in localStorage
- All vault operations (getSalt, loadVault, saveVault) now fully async

post-mortem: migration idempotency fixes + 4 new safeguards

## Migration fixes (5 files)
- All CREATE TYPE → DO/EXCEPTION blocks (idempotent on re-run)
- All CREATE TABLE → IF NOT EXISTS
- All CREATE INDEX → IF NOT EXISTS
- ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE → IF NOT EXISTS (20260224_152146)

## New safeguards
- scripts/validate-migrations.sh: Python-based pre-commit validator
  Scans up() functions for bare CREATE TYPE and CREATE TABLE without IF NOT EXISTS
- scripts/new-migration.sh: Template generator with safe boilerplate
  Starts every new migration with correct patterns pre-filled
- src/lib/validateEnv.ts: Startup env var validator
  Throws immediately if DATABASE_URI, PAYLOAD_SECRET, S3_* are missing
  Called from payload.config.ts (Railway deployment guard)
- app/api/health/route.ts: /api/health endpoint
  Checks env vars + live DB connectivity, returns 200/503

Motivated by: MQ Studio outage 2026-03-07 (Digest 2683012492)

* fix(graph3d): catch OrbitControls pointercancel crash

three.js OrbitControls.onPointerUp crashes when DragControls dispatches
a pointercancel event — the pointer is already removed from the internal
tracking Map, so accessing .x throws TypeError.

Fix: intercept pointercancel in capture phase on the container div to
prevent propagation to OrbitControls. This is a known race condition
between DragControls and OrbitControls in three.js/react-force-graph-3d.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(graph3d): cache Three.js geometries/materials to prevent WebGL crash

Each createNodeObject call was creating a unique MeshPhongMaterial +
BufferGeometry + SpriteMaterial + CanvasTexture. On graphs with 200+
nodes this exhausts the WebGL context — VALIDATE_STATUS false shader
errors → render failure → React re-render → infinite loop.

Fix:
- Cache geometries per entity type (shared across all nodes of same type)
- Cache materials per entity type + energy bucket (3 levels)
- Cache glow textures and sprite materials per color + opacity bucket
- Replace MeshPhongMaterial with MeshLambertMaterial (lighter shader)
- Add disposeNodeCaches() cleanup on component unmount
- Result: ~20 shader programs instead of ~500+ for a typical graph

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(graph3d): stop Context Lost infinite loop + fix material type mismatch

Wrap filteredNodes/filteredEdges in useMemo to prevent new array refs
every render, which caused the layout useEffect to re-fire infinitely
→ perpetual ForceGraph3D re-mount → WebGL Context Lost/Restored loop.

Also replace all MeshPhongMaterial instanceof checks with MeshLambertMaterial
to match the material type used in nodeObjects.ts (previous commit), fixing
silently broken spreading activation, heatmaps, and selection highlights.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
